definition: To deliberately avoid or keep away from
Present, appearing, or found everywhere
To shorten or condense
To reduce in force, value, or intensity
An opinion based on incomplete information; to guess

Peculiar to an individual; distinctive
A sign or measure that indicates a trend, condition, or value.
Clear or obvious; to display clearly
A person who advocates for something
To reduce in intensity or amount
To make something less severe
To make something better
Deviating from the norm; irregular
To contradict or give a false impression
Having a lot of space; roomy
To list or count one by one
Open to multiple interpretations; ambiguous
To reveal or display clearly
Out of place; not harmonious
Having many different aspects or elements
To come before in time or order
Representing the most perfect example

To transfer or reverse position
To weaken or damage gradually
To emphasize or highlight
To clear of blame; to prove right
To exist in large numbers or amounts; to be plentiful.
To assess the value or quality of something.
Wearing or grinding down by friction.
To increase or enlarge something.

Attention to all the facts and circumstances of a case; consideration of all that is pertinent.
To combine or fuse into one; to confuse or mistake one thing for another.
Marked by heated arguments or controversy.
Vast in quantity or number, profuse, abundant; taking place on a large scale.

A claim, hypothesis, or other belief that is contrary to the facts.

A scarcity or lack of something.

Causing damage or harm.

An inconsistency between facts or sentiments.
To obscure or block out; to surpass or diminish in importance.

To be a perfect example of; to represent in miniature.

To use selfishly or for profit; to take unfair advantage of.

Treating serious issues with (often deliberately) inappropriate humour; flippant.

Diversity

Domineering, arrogant, or overbearing.

To challenge or attack as false; to cast doubt upon.

To be in every part of; to spread through.
Remove the possibility of; rule out; prevent or exclude; to make impossible.

An act of retaliation.
To repeal, annul, or declare void; to take (something such as a rule or contract) out of effect.
Occurring at irregular intervals; having no set plan or order.
To replace or take the place of something, often something old or obsolete.
To take the place of; to replace, to supersede.
Stealthy, furtive, well hidden, covert (especially movements).

Proof or evidence that something exists or is true.
Unambiguous; without equivocation or ambiguity; singularly clear, unmistakable, or unquestionable
To deprive of courage, strength, or composure; to agitate or fluster.
Not able to be held, as of an opinion or position; unholdable, indefensible.
To provide lodging or space for; to adapt or adjust to fit a need.

The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions, typically in a particular domain.
Concerned with beauty or the appreciation of beauty.
A natural attraction or feeling of kinship to a person or thing.
An open or intentional offense, slight, or insult.
The act of collecting together (aggregating).
To form a line; to fall into line.
To gather together or accumulate a large quantity of something.
Open to multiple interpretations.
Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas about something or someone.
Willing to respond to persuasion or suggestions.
Lacking a definite form or clear shape.

Caused or influenced by human activity.

Of the same origin as claimed; genuine.

To grow or expand.

Uncritically satisfied with oneself or one's achievements; smug.

Based on observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic.
